records.ety: [F. prsumer, L. praesumere, praesumptum; prae before + sumere to take. See Assume, Redeem.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Presumed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Presuming.]

defs: To assume or take beforehand; esp., to do or undertake without leave or authority previously obtained.
quotes: Dare he presume to scorn us in this manner?
Bold deed thou hast presumed, adventurous Eve.

defs: To take or suppose to be true, or entitled to belief, without examination or proof, or on the strength of probability; to take for granted; to infer; to suppose.
quotes: Every man is to be presumed innocent till he is proved to be guilty.
What rests but that the mortal sentence pass, . . . Which he presumes already vain and void, Because not yet inflicted?

defs: To suppose or assume something to be, or to be true, on grounds deemed valid, though not amounting to proof; to believe by anticipation; to infer; as, we may presume too far.

To venture, go, or act, by an assumption of leave or authority not granted; to go beyond what is warranted by the circumstances of the case; to venture beyond license; to take liberties; -- often with on or upon before the ground of confidence.
quotes: Do not presume too much upon my love.
This man presumes upon his parts.
